reasoning: C had the most genuinely distinct five: each version names and attacks a different base assumption (day-as-span vs the Salt Garden's solid readable residue, single perceiver vs the Shared Pulse's distributed street-wide temperature field, the Tide Score's draining cistern raising a wire's pitch, the Animal Almanac's species-to-species biological relay), so the set spans separate conceptual spaces rather than restyling one idea. A is vivid and well-crafted but versions 4 (collective human sound) and 5 (crowd-motion clock) cluster on the same social-aggregate engine, narrowing its spread. B reads as five thin sketches: the Thermal River and Tasting Menu overlap, and the Tasting Menu never actually mechanizes a day's passage, leaving it the least developed and least bold.
